hook: Are you prepared to be monitored 24/7? Well sit down let me tell you bout what is being worked on as we speak!
novelty_element: Creator frames a surveillance/government policy topic using intimate, confrontational direct address ('sit down let me tell you') — turning a dry civic issue into a personal warning delivered as if by a trusted insider, collapsing social distance and driving share behavior as a warning-forwarding reflex.
cta: sit down let me tell you bout what is being worked on as we speak
pain_points: fear of mass surveillance without consent
epistemic pain: 'I didn't know this and I should have'
collective threat no individual can opt out of
author: pauletteonthemic

hook: AI just replaced social media managers.
novelty_element: Hook uses 'replaced' rather than 'helps' or 'automates' — frames the product as full labor substitution, triggering both fear (for social media managers) and desire (for business owners), making it shareable across two opposing audiences simultaneously.
product_in_frame: Riona — AI Agent for social media account management
cta: Implied follow/learn more via caption hashtags and author profile
pain_points: business owners cannot afford or do not want to pay a social media manager
professionals anxious about AI job displacement
need for consistent content output without full-time hire
author: nathanhodgson.ai

hook: Did Claude Code just kill marketing agencies?
novelty_element: Weaponizes a brand name (Claude Code) as agent of disruption against an entire industry — framing a tool tutorial as an industry-killing event rather than a productivity tip, triggering both fear-of-missing-out in agency owners and aspiration in solopreneurs.
product_in_frame: Claude Code (used to build a 7-agent marketing team)
cta: Watch how to build a marketing team with 7 AI Agents each specialising in a different marketing discipline
pain_points: cannot afford a full marketing team as a solopreneur
agency owners threatened by AI displacement
need professional marketing without professional budget
author: nocode.joshua

hook: Guys be careful this is so scary
novelty_element: Creator positions themselves as peer-level witness rather than expert — casual grammar signals authentic alarm, collapsing creator-viewer distance and triggering share impulse as a protective reflex toward others in viewer's network.
cta: be careful (implicit warning; no explicit product CTA)
pain_points: fear of being deceived or impersonated by AI without knowing it
universal vulnerability: anyone with voice or face online can be cloned
feeling unprepared for threats you didn't know existed
author: cottage.beansoup

hook: Some of my favorite platforms for PR
novelty_element: Frames a resource list as personal curation ('my favorites') rather than generic advice — possessive framing signals insider access and elevates perceived authority without requiring visible credentials.
cta: Lmk if you know any other ones
pain_points: aspiring influencers don't know how to approach brands for PR packages
feeling locked out of the brand-deal system
author: laviedelalou
